attacking_style: Not a personal attacker. Functions as penultimate lead-out man, releasing sprinters 200-400m from finish, though timing varies strategically by course geometry and conditions.
team_tactics: Executes Quick-Step lead-out train as penultimate rider before final launcher. Controls tempo through finale rather than pure power lead-out, deliberately stringing peloton to optimize sprinter positioning.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint finales with technical closing circuits. Adapts lead-out release point based on corner placement - releases before final corner rather than traditional latest-possible handoff to prevent wheel-surfing.
vulnerability: Distance judgment on lead-out timing - released sprinter 500m out in Paris-Nice causing premature wind exposure, suggesting pacing miscalculations on unfamiliar courses.
key_patterns: Releases sprinter earlier than traditional lead-out men, prioritizing clean acceleration over maximum speed transfer (4 observations)
Uses tempo control and peloton-stringing rather than pure power lead-out approach (3 observations)
Adapts release point based on technical features: drops before corners to prevent counterattacks (2 observations)
Functions in dual lead-out capacity, demonstrates Quick-Step sprint train depth beyond single configuration (2 observations)
Initiates winning sequences with early positioning moves, not just final lead-out execution (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Consistent lead-out execution across 2017-2018 with tactical sophistication shown in 2018 Pologne early-move initiative, but limited recent data
